MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Continued To Nov-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Form Interrogatories And Supplemental Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Feb-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Form Interrogatories And Supplemental Requests For Production Of Documents.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion of Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc. ("Uber") is GRANTED.

1) Supplemental Form Interrogatories: The court directs Plaintiff Lisa Brown to serve verified code-compliant further responses to the following Supplemental Form Interrogatories by February 26, 2021: Nos. 6.4, 6.7, 8.7, 8.8, 10.3 & 11.2. Ms. Brown's answers must be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to her permits. CCP Sec. 2030.220(a), (b). If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible. Id. Ms. Brown must state the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth in answering Uber's interrogatories. Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 76; see CCP Sec. 2023.010(f) (evasive response is ground for sanctions). In those instances where Ms. Brown asserts privilege in the course of responding to interrogatories that request her to identify documents, she must provide a description of the documents sufficient to permit evaluation of the privilege claims. (Hernandez v. Sup. Ct. (Acheson, Indus. Inc.) (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 285, 293.)

As used herein, the term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state, including, without limitation, the case decisions cited in Uber's moving papers.

2) Supplemental Request for Production of Documents: The court directs Ms. Brown to serve verified code-compliant further responses to Uber's Supplemental Request for Production of Documents by February 26, 2021 and produce all responsive non-privileged documents within her possession, custody or control by February 26, 2021. If she withholds documents on grounds of privilege or privacy, she must provide a log concurrent with her document production, including the specific grounds of each claim of privilege or privacy.

3) Sanctions: Uber's request for sanctions is denied.

Prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at pvestal@nvlawllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Motion To Quash Service Of The Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Christopher J. Cummiskey Esq., In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-17-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Christopher J. Cummiskey, Esq. of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara, LLP, T:702.258.6665, for defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
Dave Marcolini, Esq., specially appearing for plaintiff.

RULING: The tentative ruling was not contested. There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

Special Appearing DEFENDANT TEWELDE GEBREMARIAM'S Motion To Quash Service Of the Summons and Complaint on defendant Gebremariam is granted. By opposing the motion on its merits, plaintiff Lisa Brown waived her objection based on untimely service of the moving papers. (Reedy v. Bussell (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 1272, 1288). Ms. Brown failed to provide sufficient evidence to satisfy her burden to prove that service of summons and complaint made on Mr. Gebremariam was proper. The declaration of Ms. Barhe shows that she resides at the purported service address, she did not accept service for Mr. Gebremariam, Mr. Gebremariam has been in Ethiopia since September 2016, and was in Ethiopia on February 2018 when he was allegedly personally served. The proof of service dated February 9, 2018 lacks any physical description of the man that was personally served. Mr. Hutson's declaration is insufficient evidence because he states that he personally served Mr. Gebremariam or a "person authorized to receive service of process" for Mr. Gebremariam and offered no explanation as to the person "who claimed to be" Mr. Gebremariam.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
